76	// ResolveTriagePolicy returns the effective triage policy for this config,
77	// layering any `triage.policies` entries on top of the binary default
78	// ladders. Unknown failure-mode names and unknown action names are
79	// silently dropped (schema validation surfaces typos at load time).
80	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveTriagePolicy() triage.TriagePolicy {
81		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
82		if c == nil || c.Triage == nil || len(c.Triage.Policies) == 0 {
83			return policy
84		}
85		for modeStr, actionStrs := range c.Triage.Policies {
86			mode, err := triage.ParseFailureMode(modeStr)
87			if err != nil {
88				continue
89			}
90			ladder := make([]triage.Action, 0, len(actionStrs))
91			for _, a := range actionStrs {
92				act, err := triage.ParseAction(a)
93				if err != nil {
94					continue
95				}
96				ladder = append(ladder, act)
97			}
98			if len(ladder) == 0 {
99				continue
100			}
101			policy.Ladders[mode] = ladder
102		}
103		return policy
104	}

185	// Default values for WorkersConfig resolvers. These mirror the hardcoded
186	// constants the execute-loop uses today; bead 6/7 of SD-024 wires the

194	// ResolveNoProgressCooldown returns the effective no-progress cooldown for
195	// this config. Defaults to 6h when unset or unparseable.
196	func (w *WorkersConfig) ResolveNoProgressCooldown() time.Duration {
197		if w == nil || w.NoProgressCooldown == "" {
198			return defaultNoProgressCooldown
199		}
200		d, err := time.ParseDuration(w.NoProgressCooldown)
201		if err != nil || d <= 0 {
202			return defaultNoProgressCooldown
203		}
204		return d

460	// ApplyDefaults ensures all required fields have default values
461	func (c *NewConfig) ApplyDefaults() {
462		if c.Version == "" {
463			c.Version = "1.0"
464		}
465		if c.Library == nil {
466			c.Library = &LibraryConfig{
467				Path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
468				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
469					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
470					Branch: "main",
471				},
472			}
473		} else {
474			if c.Library.Path == "" {
475				c.Library.Path = ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
476			}
477			if c.Library.Repository == nil {
478				c.Library.Repository = &RepositoryConfig{
479					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
480					Branch: "main",
481				}
482			} else {
483				if c.Library.Repository.URL == "" {
484					c.Library.Repository.URL = "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library"
485				}
486				if c.Library.Repository.Branch == "" {
487					c.Library.Repository.Branch = "main"
488				}
489			}
490		}
491		if c.Bead == nil {
492			c.Bead = &BeadConfig{}
493		}
494		if c.PersonaBindings == nil {
495			c.PersonaBindings = make(map[string]string)
496		}
497		if c.UpdateCheck == nil {
498			c.UpdateCheck = &UpdateCheckConfig{
499				Enabled:   true,
500				Frequency: "24h",
501			}
502		} else {
503			if c.UpdateCheck.Frequency == "" {
504				c.UpdateCheck.Frequency = "24h"
505			}

14	// RunStateFileName is the basename of the live run-state file, relative to

20	// summary view for older operators.

22	
23	// RunStateLivenessTTL is the default expiry window written to refreshed
24	// attempt liveness records.

26	
27	// RunState is the on-disk record of an executing bead attempt. It is written at
28	// execute-bead start, refreshed while the agent runs, and removed on completion
29	// (or on orphan recovery of a crashed worker). Operators and HELIX consume this
30	// state to observe what is running without polling the bead tracker.
31	//
32	// See CONTRACT-001 §5 (Always-on runtime metrics and provenance).
33	type RunState struct {
34		BeadID       string    `json:"bead_id"`
35		AttemptID    string    `json:"attempt_id"`

44		// Candidate-cycle fields mirror cleanup metadata so operators can see
45		// which candidate phase owns a long-lived attempt worktree.

52	}

54	// runStatePath returns the absolute path of the run-state file for the
55	// project rooted at projectRoot.
56	func runStatePath(projectRoot string) string {
57		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", RunStateFileName)
58	}

60	func runStateDirPath(projectRoot string) string {
61		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", RunStateDirName)

64	func runStateAttemptPath(projectRoot, attemptID string) (string, error) {
65		name, err := runStateAttemptFileName(attemptID)
66		if err != nil {
67			return "", err
68		}
69		return filepath.Join(runStateDirPath(projectRoot), name), nil

72	func runStateAttemptFileName(attemptID string) (string, error) {
73		attemptID = strings.TrimSpace(attemptID)
74		if attemptID == "" {
75			return "", errors.New("run-state: attempt_id is empty")
76		}
77		if filepath.Base(attemptID) != attemptID || strings.ContainsAny(attemptID, `/\`) {
78			return "", fmt.Errorf("run-state: invalid attempt_id %q", attemptID)
79		}

33	func (f *CommandFactory) newCleanupCommand() *cobra.Command {
34		cmd := &cobra.Command{
35			Use:   "cleanup",
36			Short: "Inspect and clean stale DDx execution resources",
37			Long: `Inspect DDx-owned execution resources and conservatively clean stale state.
38	
39	By default, cleanup runs in dry-run mode and reports what it would remove.
40	Use --apply to remove stale DDx temp worktrees, stale run-state files, and
41	other DDx-owned scratch resources under the configured project roots.
42	
43	Examples:
44	  ddx cleanup
45	  ddx cleanup --json
46	  ddx cleanup --apply
47	  ddx cleanup --project /path/to/project --apply`,
48			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
49			RunE: f.runCleanup,
50		}
51	
52		cmd.Flags().Bool("apply", false, "Actually remove stale DDx execution resources")
53		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output the cleanup report as JSON")
54		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root to inspect (default: detected from CWD)")
55		return cmd
56	}
57	
58	func (f *CommandFactory) runCleanup(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
59		_ = args
60		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
61		apply, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("apply")
62		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
63	
64		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
65		if projectRoot == "" {
66			projectRoot = f.WorkingDir
67		}
68	
69		mgr := agent.NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &agent.RealGitOps{})
70		mgr.DryRun = !apply
71	
72		summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(cmd.Context())
73		if err != nil {
74			return err
75		}
76	
77		report := cleanupCommandReport{
78			DryRun:                      !apply,
79			ProjectRoot:                 summary.ProjectRoot,
80			TempRoot:                    summary.TempRoot,
81			ScannedTempDirs:             summary.ScannedTempDirs,
82			ScannedEvidenceDirs:         summary.ScannedEvidenceDirs,
83			CompleteEvidenceDirs:        summary.CompleteEvidenceDirs,
84			ScannedScratchDirs:          summary.ScannedScratchDirs,
85			RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs: summary.RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs,
86			RemovedRegisteredWorktrees:  summary.RemovedRegisteredWorktrees,
87			RemovedRunStateFiles:        summary.RemovedRunStateFiles,
88			RemovedScratchDirs:          summary.RemovedScratchDirs,
89			PreservedActiveScratchDirs:  summary.PreservedActiveScratchDirs,
90			BytesReclaimed:              summary.BytesReclaimed,
91			InodesReclaimed:             summary.InodesReclaimed,
92			ScratchBytesReclaimed:       summary.ScratchBytesReclaimed,
93			ScratchInodesReclaimed:      summary.ScratchInodesReclaimed,
94			Warnings:                    append([]agent.ExecutionCleanupWarning(nil), summary.Warnings...),
95			Observations:                append([]agent.ExecutionCleanupObservation(nil), summary.Observations...),
96		}
97		for _, issue := range summary.Issues {
98			if issue.Blocking {
99				report.BlockedErrors = append(report.BlockedErrors, issue)
100			}
101		}
102		if report.Warnings == nil {
103			report.Warnings = []agent.ExecutionCleanupWarning{}
104		}
105		if report.BlockedErrors == nil {
106			report.BlockedErrors = []agent.ExecutionCleanupIssue{}
107		}
108		if report.Observations == nil {
109			report.Observations = []agent.ExecutionCleanupObservation{}
110		}
111	
112		if asJSON {
113			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
114			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
115			return enc.Encode(report)
116		}
117	
118		out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
119		verb := "removed"
120		if report.DryRun {
121			verb = "would remove"
122		}
123		fmt.Fprintf(out, "cleanup: %s %d stale temp dir(s), %d registered worktree(s), %d run-state file(s), %d scratch dir(s)\n",
124			verb,
125			report.RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs,
126			report.RemovedRegisteredWorktrees,
127			report.RemovedRunStateFiles,
128			report.RemovedScratchDirs,
129		)
130		if report.BytesReclaimed > 0 || report.InodesReclaimed > 0 {
131			fmt.Fprintf(out, "cleanup: %s %d byte(s), %d inode(s)\n", verb, report.BytesReclaimed, report.InodesReclaimed)
132		}
133		if report.ScratchBytesReclaimed > 0 || report.ScratchInodesReclaimed > 0 || report.PreservedActiveScratchDirs > 0 {
134			fmt.Fprintf(out, "cleanup: scratch scope %s %d byte(s), %d inode(s); preserved %d active scratch dir(s)\n",
135				verb,
136				report.ScratchBytesReclaimed,
137				report.ScratchInodesReclaimed,
138				report.PreservedActiveScratchDirs,
139			)
140		}
141		if report.CompleteEvidenceDirs > 0 {
142			fmt.Fprintf(out, "cleanup: preserved %d complete evidence bundle(s)\n", report.CompleteEvidenceDirs)
143		}
144		for _, warning := range report.Warnings {
145			fmt.Fprintf(out, "warning: %s (%s): %s\n", warning.Path, warning.Class, warning.Message)
146		}
147		for _, issue := range report.BlockedErrors {
148			fmt.Fprintf(out, "blocked: %s (%s): %s\n", issue.Path, issue.Class, issue.Message)
149		}
150		if report.DryRun {
151			fmt.Fprintln(out, "run again with --apply to remove these resources")
152		}
153		return nil

func TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldExecutionDirs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()

	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10)
	oldDir := setupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, "20260101T000000-exec1old1", oldTime)

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})

func TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldAgentLogs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()

	logsDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "agent-logs")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(logsDir, 0o755))

	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10)
	oldLogPath := filepath.Join(logsDir, "agent-sess-old-abc.jsonl")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(oldLogPath, []byte("{}\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(oldLogPath, oldTime, oldTime))

	oldSvcLogPath := filepath.Join(logsDir, "svc-sess-old-def.jsonl")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(oldSvcLogPath, []byte("{}\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(oldSvcLogPath, oldTime, oldTime))

	nonLogPath := filepath.Join(logsDir, "sessions.jsonl")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(nonLogPath, []byte("{}\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(nonLogPath, oldTime, oldTime))

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.NoFileExists(t, oldLogPath)
	assert.NoFileExists(t, oldSvcLogPath)
	assert.FileExists(t, nonLogPath, "non-agent log files must not be pruned")
	assert.Equal(t, int64(2), summary.RemovedAgentLogs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "removed_agent_log"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldWorkerDirs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()

	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))

func TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveItems(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()

	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10)

	// Active execution dir referenced by a run state
	activeAttemptID := "20260101T000000-active1a"
	activeExecDir := setupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, activeAttemptID, oldTime)
	require.NoError(t, WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
		BeadID:    "ddx-active",
		AttemptID: activeAttemptID,
		SessionID: "session-preserve-abc",
		StartedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
	}))

	// Active agent log referenced by the live run state session
	logsDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "agent-logs")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(logsDir, 0o755))
	activeLogPath := filepath.Join(logsDir, "agent-session-preserve-abc.jsonl")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(activeLogPath, []byte("{}\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(activeLogPath, oldTime, oldTime))

	// Fresh worker dir (within retention window — mtime not past cutoff)
	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))
	freshWorkerDir := filepath.Join(workersDir, "worker-fresh-11223344")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(freshWorkerDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(freshWorkerDir, "status.json"), []byte(`{"worker_id":"worker-fresh","pid":0}`), 0o644))
	// mtime is recent (within retention), so it should not be pruned
	freshTime := time.Now()
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(freshWorkerDir, freshTime, freshTime))

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.DirExists(t, activeExecDir, "active execution dir must be preserved")
	assert.FileExists(t, activeLogPath, "active agent log must be preserved")
	assert.DirExists(t, freshWorkerDir, "fresh worker dir must be preserved")
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedEvidenceDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedAgentLogs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedWorkerDirs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_active_agent_log"))
}

data.text:
All 7 AC tests pass and lefthook is green. Done.

**Summary of changes across 4 files:**

- **`cli/internal/config/types.go`**: `ApplyDefaults` now sets `RetainDays=90` (was 7)
- **`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go`**:
  - `defaultEvidenceRetainDays` changed from 7 → 90
  - `ExecutionCleanupSummary` gains `RemovedAgentLogs` and `RemovedWorkerDirs` counters
  - `pruneAgentLogs`: prunes `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `svc-*.jsonl` older than RetainDays; skips files whose session_id appears in an active RunState
  - `pruneWorkerDirs`: prunes `.ddx/workers/<id>/` dirs older than RetainDays; skips dirs where `status.json` records a live PID
  - Both are called from `Cleanup()` after `pruneEvidenceDirs`; `RetainDays=0` disables all three loops
- **`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go`**: Renamed `TestExecutionCleanup_RetainDaysDefault7` → `TestExecutionCleanup_DefaultRetainDays90`; removed duplicate `RetainDaysZero_DisablesRetention`; added 6 new required tests
- **`cli/cmd/cleanup.go`**: `cleanupCommandReport` and text output now surface `RemovedAgentLogs` and `RemovedWorkerDirs`
